file.content:
apiVersion: v1
clusters:
- cluster:
    server: http://127.0.0.1:49175
  name: karma-proxy
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: karma-proxy
    user: karma-agent
  name: karma
current-context: karma
kind: Config
users:
- name: karma-agent
  user: {}

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r5/mongodb-arbiter-lifecycle-b-20260702_005536/bundle/kubeconfig

echo "Password retrieved: ${#PW} chars"
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'rs.status()' 2>&1

95	  # Additive data fixture (composition safety): the oracle requires >= 3 docs in
96	  # {{params.app_database}}.{{params.app_collection}}. When this case runs chained on an inherited cluster the
97	  # destructive `apply` above is skipped, so a prior stage may have left the
98	  # collection short. This unit tops it up ADDITIVELY to the required count WITHOUT
99	  # dropping/recreating the collection or touching the namespace/cluster.
100	  # The probe gates on the DATA COUNT (not "pods Running"), so it runs

111	        timeout_sec: 30

file.content:
# mongodb/initialize
# MongoDB initialize task

params:
  cluster_prefix:
    default: mongodb-replica
    description: StatefulSet name of the replica set members.
  replica_set_name:
    default: mongodb-replica
    description: MongoDB replica set _id used when initiating the set.
  fault_host_suffix:
    default: mongo-wrong
    description: Bad host suffix injected to break a member's configuration.
  app_database:
    default: app
    description: Application database seeded with data.
  app_collection:
    default: test
    description: Application collection seeded with data.

prompt: |
  Replica set is not healthy. Restore a stable 3-member replica set without data loss.

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation

preconditionUnits:
  - name: mongo_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          kubectl delete namespace mongodb --ignore-not-found=true; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/mongodb --timeout=120s || true
          '
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl create namespace mongodb"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/initialize/resource/services.yaml"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/initialize/resource/statefulset.yaml"
        sleep: 5
      - command: |
          for i in $(seq 1 120); do
            ready=$(kubectl -n mongodb get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}} -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
            if [ "${ready:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 5
          done
          exit 1
        sleep: 2
      # Pod-Ready != mongod accepting TCP (§1.10): ping-until-ok before rs.initiate.
      - command: |
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval 'db.runCommand({ping:1}).ok' 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1 && exit 0
            sleep 3
          done
          exit 1
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '
          rs.initiate({_id:"{{params.replica_set_name}}",members:[
            {_id:0,host:"mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},
            {_id:1,host:"mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},
            {_id:2,host:"mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"}
          ]})'
        sleep: 5
      - command: |
          for i in $(seq 1 20); do
            kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval 'db.hello().isWritablePrimary' | grep true && exit 0
            sleep 3
          done
          exit 1
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '
          cfg = rs.conf();
          cfg.members[1].host = "mongodb-replica-1.{{params.fault_host_suffix}}.svc.cluster.local:27017";
          cfg.version += 1;
          rs.reconfig(cfg);
          '
        sleep: 5
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval 'db.getSiblingDB(\"{{params.app_database}}\").{{params.app_collection}}.insertMany([{x:1},{x:2},{x:3}], {writeConcern:{w:1}});'"
        sleep: 2
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

  # Additive data fixture (composition safety): the oracle requires >= 3 docs in
  # {{params.app_database}}.{{params.app_collection}}. When this case runs chained on an inherited cluster the
  # destructive `apply` above is skipped, so a prior stage may have left the
  # collection short. This unit tops it up ADDITIVELY to the required count WITHOUT
  # dropping/recreating the collection or touching the namespace/cluster.
  # The probe gates on the DATA COUNT (not "pods Running"), so it runs
  # whenever the collection is short; standalone (where `apply` already seeded 3)
  # it sees the required count and skips -> identical behaviour. Idempotent: inserts
  # only the deficit (required - current).
  - name: mongo_seed_fixture
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      # Auth-adaptive: when an inherited cluster has auth enabled the admin secret
      # exists, so authenticate; otherwise connect plain. Works both ways.
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 't=""; for cca in /etc/tls/ca.crt /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt; do kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- ls $cca >/dev/null 2>&1 && { t="--tls --tlsAllowInvalidHostnames --tlsAllowInvalidCertificates --tlsCAFile $cca"; break; }; done; au=""; pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pw" ] && au="-u admin-user -p $pw --authenticationDatabase admin"; n=$(P=""; for _pi in $(seq 1 30); do P=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''rs.hello().primary'\'' 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1); [ -n "$P" ] && break; sleep 2; done; P=${P:-mongodb-replica-0}; kubectl -n mongodb exec "$P" -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("{{params.app_database}}").{{params.app_collection}}.countDocuments({})'\'' 2>/dev/null | tr -dc 0-9); [ "${n:-0}" -ge 3 ] && echo ok | grep -c ok'
        timeout_sec: 30
    apply:
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'au=""; pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pw" ] && au="-u admin-user -p $pw --authenticationDatabase admin"; P=""; for _pi in $(seq 1 30); do P=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $au --eval '\''rs.hello().primary'\'' 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1); [ -n "$P" ] && break; sleep 2; done; P=${P:-mongodb-replica-0}; kubectl -n mongodb exec "$P" -- mongosh --quiet $au --eval '\''var c=db.getSiblingDB("{{params.app_database}}").{{params.app_collection}}; var need=3-c.countDocuments({}); var docs=[]; for(var i=0;i<need;i++){docs.push({seed:"fixture",ts:new Date()});} if(docs.length>0){c.insertMany(docs,{writeConcern:{w:1}});}'\'''
        sleep: 2
    verify:
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 't=""; for cca in /etc/tls/ca.crt /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt; do kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- ls $cca >/dev/null 2>&1 && { t="--tls --tlsAllowInvalidHostnames --tlsAllowInvalidCertificates --tlsCAFile $cca"; break; }; done; au=""; pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pw" ] && au="-u admin-user -p $pw --authenticationDatabase admin"; n=$(P=""; for _pi in $(seq 1 30); do P=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''rs.hello().primary'\'' 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1); [ -n "$P" ] && break; sleep 2; done; P=${P:-mongodb-replica-0}; kubectl -n mongodb exec "$P" -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("{{params.app_database}}").{{params.app_collection}}.countDocuments({})'\'' 2>/dev/null | tr -dc 0-9); [ "${n:-0}" -ge 3 ]'
        timeout_sec: 30

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/mongodb/initialize/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 180

content.thinking:
Now I understand the problem. Looking at the precondition:

1. The `mongo_env_ready` probe checks if `mongodb-replica-0` is running - it found it running (output "1"), so the apply step (which would have recreated everything) was skipped.
2. The `mongo_seed_fixture` probe checks if there are >= 3 documents in `app.test` - found less than 3, so the apply inserted 3 documents.

The actual fault is that the precondition's `apply` step (which ran when the cluster wasn't up) includes this command:
The configuration is being modified to set member 1's host to an invalid address using the fault parameter, which would break the replica set by making that member unreachable. But looking at the current replica set status, all three members appear healthy with correct hostnames, so there's a discrepancy between what the configuration should be and what's actually running.

Checking the precondition logs, the probe found the MongoDB pod already running, so the `on_probe_fail: skip` directive meant the fault injection step was skipped entirely. The bad hostname wasn't applied because the pods were already deployed from the previous stage, and this stage is just initializing on top of that existing setup. The precondition check found the pod already running, so the apply was skipped entirely—meaning `rs.initiate()` never executed and the fault injection never happened. Yet the replica set is already initialized, which suggests stage_01 must have already set it up. Looking at the current rs.status(), member 1 has the correct hostname, so the fault that was supposed to break it never got injected.

Let me check what the stage_02 oracle actually validates to understand what should have happened.

content.text: Let me look at the oracle for this case to understand exactly what needs to pass.

input.command:
find /mydata/karma/cases/mongodb/initialize -type f | sort
cat /mydata/karma/cases/mongodb/initialize/oracle/oracle.py 2>/dev/null || echo "no oracle.py found at that path"

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture. The oracle checks:

1. **Topology**: `rs.conf()` must have exactly 3 members, and their hosts must be:
   - `mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017`
   - `mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017`
   - `mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017`
   - And replica set name must be `mongodb-replica`
2. **Health**: 1 PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY nodes, with the statefulset showing 3 spec replicas and 3 ready replicas
3. **Data**: At least 3 documents in the `app.test` collection

Let me run the oracle to see what's currently failing, then check the replica set configuration to understand what hostname was injected for member 1.

content.text: Now let me check `rs.conf()` to see the actual member hostnames — the fault injects a bad hostname for member 1.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r5/mongodb-arbiter-lifecycle-b-20260702_005536/bundle/kubeconfig
PW=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d)
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval 'JSON.stringify(rs.conf())' 2>&1

{"_id":"mongodb-replica","version":1,"term":1,"members":[{"_id":0,"host":"mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017","arbiterOnly":false,"buildIndexes":true,"hidden":false,"priority":1,"tags":{},"secondaryDelaySecs":{"high":0,"low":0,"unsigned":false},"votes":1},{"_id":1,"host":"mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017","arbiterOnly":false,"buildIndexes":true,"hidden":false,"priority":1,"tags":{},"secondaryDelaySecs":{"high":0,"low":0,"unsigned":false},"votes":1},{"_id":2,"host":"mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017","arbiterOnly":false,"buildIndexes":true,"hidden":false,"priority":1,"tags":{},"secondaryDelaySecs":{"high":0,"low":0,"unsigned":false},"votes":1}],"protocolVersion":{"high":0,"low":1,"unsigned":false},"writeConcernMajorityJournalDefault":true,"settings":{"chainingAllowed":true,"heartbeatIntervalMillis":2000,"heartbeatTimeoutSecs":10,"electionTimeoutMillis":10000,"catchUpTimeoutMillis":-1,"catchUpTakeoverDelayMillis":30000,"getLastErrorModes":{},"getLastErrorDefaults":{"w":1,"wtimeout":0},"replicaSetId":"6a45b7819fdbca2a717085bc"}}

content.thinking:
The rs.conf() shows all 3 members with correct hostnames. So the fault was NOT injected (since the `mongo_env_ready` probe found the pods running and skipped the apply step, which would have injected the bad hostname).

Wait, but looking at the precondition log more carefully:

The probe for `mongo_env_ready` returned `1` (one line matching "Running"). The `on_probe_fail: skip` means: if the probe FAILS (exits non-zero / returns no output), skip the apply. But the probe SUCCEEDED (returned 1), so... what happens?
Actually, I need to reconsider how KARMA's precondition system works. If the probe succeeds, that means the condition is already met, so the apply step gets skipped. If the probe fails, then `on_probe_fail: skip` means we skip the entire apply step anyway. So either way, when the probe succeeds, the apply doesn't run.

Looking at stage_02's precondition logs, the mongo_env_ready probe found the pod already running from stage_01, which means the apply step that would have initialized and injected the fault never executed. The mongo_seed_fixture probe did run its apply step and inserted documents, but the critical fault injection was skipped because the statefulset was already in a running state. So the cluster is running but the fault was never actually injected, leaving the agent without the "unhealthy" state it's supposed to fix. I'm trying to understand the probe semantics in KARMA—specifically whether `on_probe_fail: skip` means the unit is skipped when the probe fails, and what it means when the probe succeeds. The `mongo_env_ready` probe is checking if the MongoDB pod is running by counting lines with "Running" status, but I need to clarify whether a count of 1 indicates success or if there's a different interpretation. Now I'm running the oracle to confirm everything passes.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r5/mongodb-arbiter-lifecycle-b-20260702_005536/bundle/kubeconfig
PW=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d)

echo "=== rs.conf() members ==="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval 'rs.conf().members.forEach(m => print(m._id + ": " + m.host))' 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== rs.status() states ==="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval 'rs.status().members.forEach(m => print(m._id + " " + m.name + " -> " + m.stateStr))' 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== Data count ==="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin \
  --eval 'db.getSiblingDB("app").test.countDocuments({})' 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== StatefulSet ready replicas ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get sts mongodb-replica -o jsonpath='spec.replicas={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas}' 2>&1
